text:
Market today: Mixed-to-cautious mood. Micron's blowout earnings are lighting up chip/semi sentiment in premarket — that's a direct tailwind for your AVGO and SMH names today. But JPMorgan's warning that retail is pulling back from margin and options is the real undercurrent, and that's a headwind for high-beta tech like yours.

Your holdings: PLTR is your loudest alarm — down 12.77% and on a SELL signal; no catalyst today to save it, and margin makes that hole deeper fast. AVGO is your biggest position, also down 12.45% on a TRIM signal — Micron's pop might give it a bounce, but that's a gift to reduce size, not hold and hope. SPAL is down 14.48% with 2x leverage and decay baked in — it bleeds even sideways; your stop at $19 is close, treat it as live.

Watch: 1) Does Micron's rally actually lift AVGO and SMH in regular hours, or does it fade? 2) JPMorgan's margin-caution note — if retail keeps pulling back, high-beta tech sells off harder.

Bottom line: Use any Micron-driven bounce in semis to execute your PLTR sell and AVGO trim — don't let a green open talk you into holding losers on margin.
